pending.body: Fix the post-reconciliation/post-sync selecting audit trail. `select_next_milestone_bead_from_recommendation` and `select_next_milestone_bead` both call `sync_controller_state(... Selecting ...)` in `src/cli/run.rs:694-703` and `src/cli/run.rs:730-739`, but the real call sites already moved the controller to `Selecting` in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:188-199,239-253` and `src/cli/run.rs:1310-1330`. Because `sync_controller_state` checkpoints same-state requests instead of recording the new reason (`src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:620-623`), the journal never captures the new “requesting the next bead recommendation…” / “validating a pre-fetched…” reasons on the production paths. Either transition through a distinct state or add a same-state path that updates the reason/journal entry so operators can see the actual selection step.

Normalize bead IDs in restart-time ready checks. The new selection code added qualified/unqualified matching helpers in `src/cli/run.rs:272-296`, but `ProjectMilestoneControllerRuntime::has_ready_beads` still does a raw `planned_refs.contains(bead.id)` at `src/cli/run.rs:938-944`. `resume_controller` now relies on that method to keep `Selecting` controllers active and to unblock `Blocked` controllers (`src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:815-824` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:967-975`). If `br ready` and the milestone plan use different bead-id forms, a restart will incorrectly report “no ready beads” even though the fresh selection path would find one. Reuse `milestone_planned_refs_contain`/canonicalization here and add a restart test for the mixed-ID case.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 7 -> Plan and Implement
